cityPages.statementText: Sometimes, having someone genuinely listen is what makes the difference

cityPages.title: Online Therapy in Newcastle
cityPages.metaDescription: Online counselling for people in Newcastle. BACP-accredited therapy from home. Warm, confidential, and completely at your pace.
cityPages.heading: Online Therapy in Newcastle
cityPages.intro: Newcastle is a proud, lively city, but behind the friendly exterior, many people are dealing with real struggles. If you’re in Newcastle and finding things tough, online therapy offers a way to get professional support without the barriers.

sections.heading: Pressures of life in Newcastle
paragraphs: Newcastle has a strong sense of community, but the North East also faces some of the highest rates of mental health difficulties in the country. Economic challenges, limited local mental health provision, and the pressures of student life all play a part.

You might be struggling with anxiety, depression, relationship issues, or simply feeling stuck. Whatever you’re going through, you don’t have to face it alone, and you don’t need to wait until it feels like a crisis to ask for help.

sections.heading: Therapy without the wait
paragraphs: Online therapy means you can skip the NHS waiting list and access support now. Sessions are by Zoom or phone, so you can talk from the comfort of your own home, wherever you are in Newcastle.
I’m Harriet, a BACP-accredited person-centred counsellor. I offer a free 15-minute phone consultation before we begin. It’s just a chance for us to chat, no pressure at all.

cityPages.ctaText: If you’re in Newcastle and ready to talk, I’m here to listen.
cityPages.illustrationAlt: Illustration of a person in calm reflection
cityPages.statementText: You don’t have to face it alone

cityPages.title: Online Therapy in Brighton
cityPages.metaDescription: Online therapy for people in Brighton. BACP-accredited counselling from home. A safe, affirming space to talk about what you're going through.
cityPages.heading: Online Therapy in Brighton
cityPages.intro: Brighton is known for its openness and creativity, but that doesn’t mean life here is always easy. If you’re living in Brighton and struggling with your mental health, online therapy gives you a private, flexible way to get the support you deserve.

sections.heading: The reality behind the Brighton lifestyle
paragraphs: Brighton attracts people who value individuality and freedom, but the city also has real challenges, including high rents, seasonal work patterns, and the pressure to live up to a carefree image. It’s a city where many people are dealing with things beneath the surface.
Whether you’re navigating identity questions, dealing with the after-effects of difficult experiences, or simply feeling like something isn’t right, your feelings are valid and they deserve space.

sections.heading: A safe space from home
paragraphs: Online therapy means you don’t need to search for a local therapist or sit on a waiting list. You can access support from your living room, your bedroom, or wherever you feel most at ease.
I’m Harriet, a BACP-accredited person-centred counsellor. My approach is warm, affirming, and completely non-judgemental. I offer a free 15-minute phone consultation to start.

cityPages.ctaText: If you’re in Brighton and need someone to talk to, I’d love to hear from you.

cityPages.title: Online Therapy in Cambridge
cityPages.metaDescription: Online therapy for people in Cambridge. BACP-accredited counselling from home. A supportive space for students, professionals, and anyone who needs to talk.
cityPages.heading: Online Therapy in Cambridge
cityPages.intro: Cambridge is a city of high achievers, but behind the academic excellence, many people are struggling with the pressure to perform. If you’re living in Cambridge and finding things difficult, online therapy offers a space where you can be honest about how you’re really feeling.

sections.heading: The weight of high expectations
paragraphs: Cambridge’s academic and professional culture can create intense pressure to succeed. Imposter syndrome, perfectionism, and burnout are incredibly common, but rarely talked about openly. The feeling that everyone around you is coping brilliantly can make your own struggles feel shameful.

Whether you’re a student, a researcher, or a professional, the demands of life in Cambridge can leave you feeling exhausted, anxious, or disconnected from the things that matter. That’s not a personal failing. It’s a natural response to an intense environment.

sections.heading: Confidential support, from home
paragraphs: Online therapy means you can access support discreetly and privately, without anyone needing to know. Sessions are by Zoom or phone, so you can talk from wherever feels safest.
I’m Harriet, a BACP-accredited person-centred counsellor. I offer a free 15-minute phone consultation to start. Just a chance for us to chat and see if we’re a good fit.

cityPages.ctaText: If the pressure is getting to you, you don’t have to carry it alone. I’m here.
cityPages.illustrationAlt: Illustration of a person meditating peacefully
cityPages.statementText: That’s not a personal failing. It’s a natural response to an intense environment.

cityPages.title: Online Therapy in Oxford
cityPages.metaDescription: Online counselling for people in Oxford. BACP-accredited therapy from home. A safe, non-judgemental space for anyone who needs to talk.
cityPages.heading: Online Therapy in Oxford
cityPages.intro: Oxford is a city steeped in tradition and achievement, but that legacy of excellence can weigh heavily. If you’re living in Oxford and struggling with stress, anxiety, or low mood, online therapy offers a way to get support that truly fits your life.

sections.heading: Living up to Oxford’s expectations
paragraphs: Whether you’re a student, an academic, or someone working in Oxford’s competitive professional landscape, the pressure to perform can be relentless. Imposter syndrome, burnout, and perfectionism are incredibly common in environments like this, but admitting you’re struggling can feel impossible.
The cost of living in Oxford adds another layer of stress, particularly for younger people. Financial pressure combined with academic or career demands can leave you feeling overwhelmed and isolated.

sections.heading: Private, judgement-free therapy
paragraphs: Online therapy gives you a space to be completely honest, without worrying about running into someone you know in a waiting room. You can talk from the privacy of your own home, at a time that works for you.
I’m Harriet, a BACP-accredited person-centred counsellor. I offer a free 15-minute phone consultation before we start. It’s just a relaxed chat, no commitment, no pressure.

cityPages.ctaText: If you’re in Oxford and need someone to talk to, get in touch.
cityPages.illustrationAlt: Illustration of a person in calm reflection
cityPages.statementText: You don’t have to carry it alone
